draft:
To: Hiring Manager
Subject: Backend Engineer (Python & APIs) - recommended shortlist

Hi,

I screened all 25 applicants against the job description and the weighted rubric
(Python depth 35%, production impact 30%, data & APIs 20%, stability 15%), and applied
the hard rule: 3+ years of backend Python AND cloud experience.

Recommended for interview (top 5):
  1. Mei Lin Tan - Senior Software Engineer (score 88.5/100). Seven years across backend Python and data platforms, with clear production impact.
  2. Aarav Sharma - Senior Backend Engineer (score 88.5/100). Backend engineer with six years building payment and API services in Python. Owns systems end to end.
  3. Daniel Okafor - Backend Engineer (score 88.5/100). Five years building reliable Python APIs for high-volume consumer apps, cloud-native.
  4. Emily Carter - Backend Engineer (score 88.5/100). Python backend engineer, five years, strong on APIs, data, and cloud. Ships and owns.
  5. Ananya Iyer - Backend Engineer (score 88.5/100). Backend developer, four years, Python and data-heavy APIs with measurable wins.

Flagged for your judgement (11): candidates who missed the hard rule, are
over-qualified, or have gaps in their history. I have not dropped them - they are listed
in the notebook for you to review.

This is a recommendation, not a decision. Nothing has been sent to any candidate.
Tell me which five to invite and I'll prepare the outreach.

- Recruitment agent (draft for your approval)
